_comment: Cookie — Halfling Ankuspawn Dancer. Starter campaign levels 1-10. Generated by generate_starter_campaign.py, creative fill by DM.
_narrative_summary: Cookie left Kettlebrook with five gold and a halfling's stubborn certainty that she could sing for her supper. Her first week in Varenholm reshaped her: Academy enrollment under Professor Ashworth, V.E.A. registration, a textile merchant named Torren charmed silly by the first Heartstring she'd ever consciously thrown, and a debut at The Starling that put her on the heartland performance map. By the end of week one she had Pretty Privilege turning quest payouts into bigger payouts, an Anklet of Unarmed Combat that made her kicks weigh like warhorses, a Healer's Ring from the same Pale Lantern enchanter, and four custom Gilded Thread outfits. The girl who'd planned to busk for room and board was suddenly leveling on Performance checks alone.

The middle of the campaign was Silka. The dark-haired Academy classmate who sang lullabies that made rooms forget how to breathe — secretly Lyssa Vane's Ankuspawn 'daughter,' her gold-flecked eyes hidden behind a silver suppression chain that Lyssa had told her was protection. Cookie figured out the chain on Day 8 and shattered it with Dance of Dispel during Halbert's funeral. The same day, Senna Dawnmere — Diamond-tier Wardbreaker, cross-campaign anchor from Kenji's books — clocked Cookie's Ember from across the room and locked a paternity question for the Sunken Playhouse trip. By Day 9, Cookie was Level 10, capped at maximum starter Ember strength, contracted with the Wardbreakers (Senna, Finch, Varn) at Bronze rate, and walking a marsh causeway toward a half-flooded amphitheatre where Lyssa's last lieutenant Eira had fled.

Days 9-18 closed the Varenholm starter arc in two chapters. 'The Reader' (Days 9-11): Eira surrendered at the Sunken Playhouse, signed the deposition naming Lyssa's patron only as 'the Reader,' gave up the Stormhaven courier route and Pale Lantern dropbox. Cookie confirmed Kenji as her father to Senna — refiled from client to Wardbreaker dependent. Capstone signed. Silka opened on partial sabbatical, gold rising into visible skin. 'From Kettlebrook' (Days 11-18): Day 12 a charm-tagged Crown Quarter regular tried Fern's address; Fern blocked it; Cookie pigeoned warnings to Tomas Wren in Kettlebrook (Daisy secured) and to Hazel's noble-women circuit (watch posted). Lyssa retaliation stalled at 85%. Day 14 Cookie wrote her own original recital piece — refused to play any of it for Ashworth in advance. Day 18 Civic Auditorium graduation recital: 240 humanoids, Council bench, Bardic Master from Ashenmere. Performance d20+9 ADV + d12 = 28 vs DC 22 (margin +6). Full Ember resonance bloom on the final chorus. Standing ovation. Ashworth signed Master of Voice credential, touring unrestricted, Ashenmere guest residency offered. Currently: Day 18 evening, Civic Auditorium backstage, Silka in the stagedoor wing waiting. Vess private word booked tomorrow. Three open paths going forward — Silka truth, Vess+Ashenmere political cover, or defy Ashworth's embargo and hit the Reader's dropbox tonight.

_chapter_history.title: First Night in Varenholm
_chapter_history.summary: Cookie arrived in Varenholm. Enrolled at Academy (1 GP tuition). Registered V.E.A. performer stamp (5 SP). Bought 4 luxury performance outfits at The Gilded Thread (1 GP, pickup Day 4). Booked a performance slot at The Starling (4 days out). Went to The Gilt Lily inn. Targeted textile merchant Torren — channeled Heartstring (happiness/excitement), he failed WIS save by 9. Got free dinner, secured shopping date at Maeven's for Day 2. Torren walked her home like a gentleman. Fern (roommate) wants all the gossip. Long rest — leveled to 2, learned Protector's Surge.

_chapter_history.title: The Indigo Hustle
_chapter_history.summary: Fern makeover morning — Cookie tried to give Fern a new look but botched a WIS check. Went to Maeven's tailoring shop with Torren for shopping date. Cookie seduced Torren in the dressing room while being fitted for an indigo dress (Performance check). Got Fern hired by Torren — new beauty supply department at 10x salary after Cookie demonstrated Pretty Privilege leverage. Torren agreed to act as Cookie's promoter, reaching out to patrons for the Starling show. Cookie went to the Adventurer's Guild, registered as support healer. Performed an inspiring rally (Performance check, advantage from expensive clothes) — recruited ~30 adventurers for a bandit camp raid. Cleared the bandit camp — Cookie debuted Dancer's Tai Chi in real combat. 6 successful combat Performance rolls. Collected 1 GP bounty. Long rest — leveled from 2 to 4. Unlocked: Healing Dance (L2 spell), Pretty Privilege (perk), Ember Enhancement (10x buffs), Combat Magnetism, Dance Inspiration, Dancer's Tai Chi.
_chapter_history.rolls: Multiple successes: Performance checks (seduction, guild rally, 6x combat Tai Chi), all successful. Dance Inspiration d12 applied to dancing checks.

_chapter_history.title: Golden Returns
_chapter_history.summary: Cookie gathered her adventure party (Dorith, Dalla, Silas, Marta, Garron) at the guild. Took the Missing Survey Stones quest from Alderman Edric Tosse (male). Marta tracked cart ruts to a farmstead. Found all 3 stones at Castor's farm — he'd stolen them for a wall after losing land to a boundary resurvey. Cookie tried to advocate for Castor but failed (Persuasion 12 vs DC 14). Castor fined 2 GP + 30 days labor. Pretty Privilege turned Cookie's 6 SP quest share into 6 GP from the male alderman. Visited The Pale Lantern (Isolde's enchantment shop, Artisan's Quarter). Negotiated 85 GP custom earrings (+2 CHA, adv Persuasion & Performance) down to 4 GP by offering to wear them at The Starling show — Isolde gets free advertising. Leveled to 5 on long rest.

_chapter_history.title: The Bat Dance
_chapter_history.summary: Cookie's first day at Varenholm Academy. Ashworth's Foundations of Performance class — Cookie performed Dragon's Fire (Ignis song) with a belly-dance routine, Heartstring shifted 9/15 students. Ashworth wrote something in her ledger, gave no feedback. Picked up 4 outfits from Gilded Thread. Visited Pale Lantern — earrings not ready until Day 5. Went to Adventurer's Guild — Gruff offered 3 quests, Cookie chose Academy bounty (Ashworth's fieldwork assessment at the Burnt Amphitheatre). Adventure party (Dorith, Dalla, Silas, Marta, Garron) took the livestock kills quest instead. Cookie joined Team Two: Silka (dark-haired lullaby singer — SECRET ANKUSPAWN, Lyssa's spy), Ivor (lute boy, 3 cantrips), Fenella (shortbow, gamekeeper's daughter). At the Burnt Amphitheatre, Cookie invented echolocation tap-dance (Performance 38, max d12) — used sound to map entire underchamber system without entering. Ivor's Minor Illusion visualized the echoes as a golden 3D map. Detected 2 phase spiders (CR 3) nesting in the resonance chamber. Team Four heading toward the spiders unaware. Chapter ends on cliffhanger.

_chapter_history.title: The Four Seasons of Love
_chapter_history.summary: Cookie updated her Adventurer's Guild registration (Dancer — support/tank/healer/buffer). Took bridge troll shakedown quest with Davan (greatsword), Aveline (archer), Jessamine (druid). Cookie solved the ogre non-lethally — Performance DC 18 (nat 20, 40 total) seduced the ogre via Heartstring + Ember Enhancement, then Persuasion DC 14 convinced him to let people cross and hand out pamphlets for the Starling show. Leveled to 6 from bridge Performance domain bonus. Evening: performed Ignis's Four Seasons of Love at The Starling to 200+ audience. Four songs, four outfits (black/blue/white/red), four emotions (grief/longing/lust/true love), all successful Performance checks with domain bonus each. Leveled to 7 during Song 3 (WHITE). Standing ovation. Starling management comped food/drink and offered 3 return show slots. Torren locked 3 patron inquiries. Isolde watched from back corner — earring investment validated. Cookie achieved LOCAL SENSATION fame in Varenholm after 5 days.

_chapter_history.title: Gold in the Morning
_chapter_history.summary: Cookie woke up a LOCAL SENSATION after the Starling show. Picked up bridge quest payout (8 SP, Pretty Privilege via Gruff). Attended Ashworth's second class — performed Kettlebrook lullaby (Performance 32 vs DC 16, Ember resonance pinged Silka). Questioned Ashworth about graduation requirements (3 terms: Interpretation/Adaptation/Creation, 40 days each, panel recitals). Negotiated accelerated graduation — pitched Starling as Term 1 recital, offered Ashworth credit for revolutionary program, threatened to trash curriculum if refused. Failed first counter (Persuasion 17 vs DC 18) but succeeded on ultimatum walk-away (nat 20, Persuasion 29 vs DC 22). Ashworth conceded: Starling counts as Term 1, Term 2 fieldwork assigned same day. Assignment: perform at dockworker Halbert's funeral procession (Day 7, 14:00, canal district). Visited widow Maret on Brewer's Row — learned Halbert's story (sang badly while cooking, favorite color river green, wanted kids to remember him happy). Bought mourning dress (5 CP). Leveled to 8.

_chapter_history.title: Daddy's Girl
_chapter_history.summary: Cookie performed at Halbert's funeral (canal district, vocal only, no dance — mourning dress, barefoot, river song adapted from dock ballads). Performance 23 vs DC 14 (lowered from 18 by prep visit to Maret). 7,250 XP (1,500 skill + 5,750 domain). Walked to Adventurer's Guild. Updated registration to Level 8 Dancer, listed for hire at 5 GP/quest. The Wardbreakers (Senna Dawnmere, Finch, Varn, Thessaly) — Diamond-tier Vanguard Hall, cross-campaign NPCs from Kenji Books 2-4 �� were at a corner table. Senna felt Heartstring and recognized the Ember signature from 25 years around AnkuNyx. Thessaly's crystal focus reacted to Cookie's arcane signature. Varn experienced Combat Magnetism pull. Cookie ignored all of them, sat next to Finch (fellow halfling), flirted with him, then dropped the bombshell: 'You one of the ladies that bang my dad?' — implying AnkuNyx/Kenji is her father. Chapter ends on cliffhanger — Senna's response pending.

_chapter_history.title: Six Gold and a Stew
_chapter_history.summary: Senna Dawnmere replied to Cookie's paternity claim by confirming a single past encounter with AnkuNyx 'before he knew what he was' and demanding Cookie say it again — slow. Cookie deflected with a public Ankuspawn admission to the entire Adventurer's Guild and pivoted to support-for-hire posting. A scholar-robed scrivener in the contracts alcove (suspected Cult-of-Anku informant) noted four small letters in his ledger margin without looking up. Cookie took a generous draw of honey mead, failed her WIS save (Halfling Luck reroll 8 vs DC 12), gained BUZZED. Selwyn + Beldra of Greyrush iron crew offered a 2-day flooded-mill contract; Cookie declined cleanly for school, hook open at the Hen & Hammer. Cookie crossed to the contract board buzzed, Investigation DC 13 success surfaced 3 same-day options. Jareth (guild runner, noble salon circuit) volunteered the Calverton engagement-party lead — 12 GP + tips. Cookie picked the Gilded Thread cellar wererat job instead. Brass Whistle silver-tier crew (Falconer ranger, Tova dwarf fighter, Mograth orc brawler) joined for 4-way split (1.5 GP each). Cellar combat: 8 wererats. Cookie opened with Stunning Kicks — 8/8 hits, all stunned 2 rounds, grapple broken. Falconer 2 nat-20 crits Round 1. Tai Chi kick on F1 → first FATALITY (bludgeoning paste, indigo silk shroud). Tai Chi kick on M1 (the grappler) → second FATALITY (paste-gore, brick crater). R2 saved stun Round 4, fled, killed by Falconer arrow + fall + Mograth coup-de-grace. Ankuspawn supernatural-resilience confirmed: lycanthropy curse rejected outright. Hazel paid 6 GP, threw in lifetime 10% retail discount + free first-fitting + voluntary upper-tier women's circuit promotion (Lady Calverton tier). Calverton engagement DECLINED. Cookie went home to Millward Chandlery, ate Fern's stew, slept by 21:00. Ambush check NAT 20 — quiet night. Long rest completed. Day 8 dawn 05:30, ready for 08:00 Academy class. Level 8→10 (starter cap). Ember Enhancement reached growth ceiling — story-trigger gate now in effect.

active_perks.name: Bard School Prodigy
active_perks.earned: Day 18 — Varenholm starter arc completion (Civic Auditorium graduation recital, 'From Kettlebrook,' Master of Voice credential signed by Ashworth, unanimous Council review, Bardic College of Ashenmere residency offered).
active_perks.summary: Cookie's name precedes her across the heartland — adventurer parties pay double the normal support-rate to hire her, but Cult of Anku operatives know her location whenever she's in a town.
active_perks.mechanical_effect: FAME: Cookie's reputation now extends throughout the heartland and adjacent regions (Varenholm + ~200 mile radius minimum, expanding as she travels). NPCs in any populated area have heard 'From Kettlebrook' or know her exploits before meeting her — Heartstring meets pre-existing recognition for an additive social bonus on first contact. HIRE PREMIUM: Adventurer parties on the Guild contract board offer 2x the standard support-class hire rate for her services (was 5 GP Bronze; now 10 GP base). High-tier parties may negotiate higher. CULT TRACKING: Whenever Cookie sleeps or spends >2 hours in a town or city, Cult of Anku operatives can locate her within 24 hours. The fame is the homing beacon. Mitigated by: Ember Shade ward equivalent (suppresses), deliberate disguise + alias (DC 18 Deception to maintain), or staying outside populated areas. Disabled if Cookie loses the title (unlikely — fame doesn't unwind once earned).
active_perks.design_note: Campaign-completion reward, double-edged. Upside: economic, social, narrative. Downside: the Cult now has a passive locate-by-fame on her in any town. The Reader patron-pipeline now feeds back into Cult deployment with Cookie's current location attached. Treat the Cult tracking as an at-will background threat that gates how long Cookie can stay anywhere quietly.

active_perks.name: Heartstring
active_perks.summary: Always-on Ember aura — every humanoid who sees or hears Cookie makes WIS DC 12 or is fascinated for the scene.
active_perks.mechanical_effect: Passive aura. Any humanoid within audible/visual range makes a WIS DC 12 save on first contact each scene. On fail, target is fascinated/biased favorably toward Cookie. On success, target consciously holds against the pull. Disabled only if Ember is fully nullified. Foundation for The Great User and Fans Out of Control.
active_perks.design_note: DM rolls WIS DC 12 the first time each humanoid encounters Cookie in a scene; pass/fail determines whether downstream perks fire on that NPC.

active_perks.name: Ember Last Stand
active_perks.summary: When Cookie is prone or paralyzed, humanoid enemies mock (female) or attempt to grapple (male) instead of killing — primal Ember defense.
active_perks.mechanical_effect: TRIGGER: Cookie is prone or paralyzed AND Ember active AND enemies are humanoid. FEMALE: skip kill action, mock/taunt instead. MALE: STR save vs Cookie's spell DC or spend turn attempting to grapple/mate. On failed save, male grapples; Cookie must escape or be rescued. Does NOT work on non-humanoids/undead/constructs/charm-immune. Disabled if Ember nullified.
active_perks.design_note: Last-ditch biological defense. Outcomes are degrading but Cookie survives.

active_perks.name: The Great User
active_perks.summary: Heartstring-touched humanoids bifurcate by gender × disposition: hostile females antagonize, friendly males protect (can intercept attacks as auto-crits on themselves).
active_perks.mechanical_effect: FEMALE + felt Heartstring + disposition <= neutral: antagonistic — challenges, betrays, steals admirers. MALE + disposition >= neutral: protective — buys Cookie things; in combat may intercept any attack aimed at her, counted as auto-crit on the intercepting NPC. DM checks this every Heartstring-touched scene.

active_perks.name: Fans Out of Control
active_perks.summary: Ember-amplified Great User: hostile males stalk, hostile females assassinate, friendly males form a secret guard network — Cookie rolls ambush even when sleeping safely.
active_perks.mechanical_effect: MALE + disposition < neutral + felt Heartstring: stalker, kidnap or sleep-impregnation attempts. FEMALE + felt Heartstring: assassins. MALE + disposition >= neutral + felt Heartstring: secret guard network — quietly watches, intervenes if Cookie is ambushed. EFFECT: Cookie rolls ambush even in safe sleeping locations, but always has a nearby protector. Disabled if Ember nullified.

active_perks.name: Low-Wisdom Loss-of-Control Quirk
active_perks.summary: WIS 7 turns alcohol/drugs into real risk events — failed save means the DM takes the wheel, and a blackout fires an attempt scene.
active_perks.mechanical_effect: Alcohol/drugs offered → WIS save (DC 12 baseline, scales with potency) to refuse. Fail = player loses control of Cookie. If drugged/blacked out: attempt scene fires (female aggressor = assassination, male aggressor = impregnation). Great User intervention: nearby friendly Heartstring-touched male may thwart. Thwart fail → prone → Ember Last Stand triggers. DM MUST roll the WIS save, not narrate around it.

active_perks.name: Combat Magnetism / Ember Enhancement Combat
active_perks.summary: In combat, humanoid males want to capture Cookie (last target, WIS DC 12 to attack lethally); humanoid females target her first — neutral or hostile females want her dead.
active_perks.mechanical_effect: MALES: Cookie is their LAST target; must pass WIS DC 12 to attack anyone else lethally or to attack Cookie lethally instead of capturing. FEMALES: Cookie is their FIRST target. Females with disposition <= neutral are massively jealous, prioritize killing Cookie. Females with disposition >= friendly may hesitate, switch sides, or refuse to fight. Non-humanoids unaffected. Disabled if Ember nullified.

active_perks.name: Dance Dance Revolution (DDR)
active_perks.summary: Dance-spell concentration uses CHA instead of CON — grace replaces grit for holding magic together.
active_perks.mechanical_effect: During any dance ability, Cookie's concentration checks key off CHA. Applies to Healing Dance, Dance of Dispel, Planar Waltz, Dragon's Roar Dance, and any future dance-concentration spells.

Lyssa retaliation.description: Day 12 attempt on Fern blocked — Crown Quarter regular tried to walk her home with the wrong warmth, Fern recognized it, yelled for the Millward stable boys, attacker left without confirming her address. Cookie pigeon-warned Tomas Wren who pulled Daisy into the inn back-rooms for four days. Hazel's noble-women circuit watching Daisy's road. Lyssa's people reported Cookie's network active; retaliation STALLED but not resolved. Day 18 graduation has now formally elevated Cookie under Council + Bardic College attention — direct attacks on her become much costlier for Lyssa. Retaliation now likely shifts to indirect / informational vectors (Reader correspondence, Glamour-tagged proxies, long-game).
Lyssa retaliation.trigger: Lyssa pivots from civilian-circle attack to indirect attrition — possibly a feeder leak to the Reader's pipeline naming Daisy or Tomas Wren as backup targets.
Lyssa retaliation.next_move: Lyssa likely sends a diversion through the Reader's dropbox to test whether the Pale Lantern channel is being watched. If unwatched, full names follow.

Sunken Playhouse mission.description: RESOLVED Day 10 dusk. Eira surrendered at the proscenium pillar, signed Guild deposition naming the patron 'the Reader.' Chain-fragments recovered. Two charmed villagers freed. Returned to Varenholm; Gruff stamped contract complete.

Paternity question (Senna's term).description: RESOLVED Day 10 — Cookie confirmed Kenji as father to Senna under contract terms at the Sunken Playhouse pillar. Senna refiled: Bronze contract closed, Wardbreaker dependent status begins (paperwork pending in Varenholm).
Paternity question (Senna's term).trigger: Resolved. Cross-campaign anchor preserved and reinforced — Senna-and-Wardbreakers now treat Cookie as family-adjacent rather than hire-rate client.

Silka chain collapse.description: Day 18 evening — Silka's gold visibly bloomed above her collar during Cookie's recital. Ashworth's daily check-ins through Days 11-18 documented progressive Ankuspawn marker emergence. Silka still does not know she's Ankuspawn. She knows something is happening to her body and she trusts Cookie + Ashworth more than Lyssa now. Standing in the stagedoor wing post-recital, eyes wet, waiting for Cookie.
Silka chain collapse.trigger: Silka realizes what she is, what Lyssa is, and breaks. Major story beat — sister-recognition scene with Cookie.

Cult of Anku patron exposure.description: Day 18 graduation recital made Cookie a Bard School Prodigy — Council-recognized, Bardic College of Ashenmere endorsed, name traveling the heartland. Fame is now a tracking beacon: Cult operatives know her location whenever she's in a town. The Reader's pipeline has full operational context (name, face, fame profile, regular venues). Eira's Day 10 deposition still stands; the Pale Lantern dropbox is still operational. Direct Cult engagement is now a question of when, not if.
Cult of Anku patron exposure.trigger: Cult of Anku operative (Ember Shade ward bearer) appears in Cookie's vicinity. 20 ft suppression field threat. The first such operative will arrive within 1-3 days of Cookie settling in any populated area, sooner in Stormhaven or Crown Quarter Varenholm.
Cult of Anku patron exposure.next_move: An Ember Shade ward-bearer dispatched from Stormhaven via Cult cell network. ETA dependent on Cookie's next location.

Accelerated graduation.description: RESOLVED Day 18 evening. Performance roll 23 + d12(5) = 28 vs DC 22 (margin +6). Full Heartstring resonance bloom on the final chorus; 240-person standing ovation; Vess + Bardic Master of Voice in attendance. Ashworth signed the gold-thread parchment backstage: Master of Voice credential, touring eligibility unrestricted, Ashenmere Bardic College guest residency offered.
Accelerated graduation.trigger: RESOLVED — Cookie is graduated. The Varenholm starter arc closes.

_story_engine_state._triggers_doc: Structured trigger definitions for engine consumption. Engine method get_active_triggers(action, phase, character_level) returns matching triggers; fire_trigger(trigger_id, scene_context) executes the rolls/effects. The class_features array below retains prose for AI/DM narrative reference. New mechanics MUST be encoded here as data, not as English in mechanical_effect strings.

triggers.name: Dance Dance Revolution — concentration swap
triggers.narrative_description: When concentrating on a dance ability, the swirl of movement and breath replaces the gut-clench focus most casters need. Cookie holds her concentration through grace, not grit.

triggers.name: Dance Dance Revolution — Ember charm projection
triggers.narrative_description: While Cookie is mid-cast on a dance ability, the Ember turns the choreography into a hostile-gaze redirect. Half the time enemies forget Cookie exists and start swinging at each other instead. Effect ends the moment the dance ends.

triggers.name: Heartstring — passive emotional resonance
triggers.narrative_description: Cookie's voice and movement carry a resonance her listeners' bodies answer before their minds do. Listeners who fail their will-shaped resistance feel what Cookie feels — joy, grief, lust, focus, whatever the room is — for as long as Cookie is broadcasting.

triggers.name: Combat Magnetism — humanoid enemy behavior
triggers.narrative_description: Cookie's Ankuspawn beauty plus the Ember's broadcast hijack humanoid combat instincts. Males want to capture, not kill — she's their last lethal target unless they pass a will-save. Females register her as the first threat: jealousy if their disposition is neutral-or-below, hesitation if it's already friendly.

triggers.name: Pretty Privilege — 10x gold multiplier on male humanoid payments
triggers.narrative_description: Male humanoids over-tip, over-pay, and over-compensate when interacting with Cookie. Engine multiplies any gold-amount earned in such transactions by 10x. Does not apply to female NPCs. Applies retroactively if gender becomes known after payment.

triggers.name: Ember Enhancement — 10x buff/heal/emotion-transfer multiplier
triggers.narrative_description: All buff spells, healing spells, and emotion-transfer effects originating from Cookie are amplified by 10x while Ember is active. Suppressed by Ember-Shade wards or Ember nullification.

triggers.name: The Great User — gender x disposition behavior routing
triggers.narrative_description: Heartstring-touched humanoids bifurcate by gender × disposition. Female ≤ neutral becomes antagonistic. Male ≥ neutral becomes protective and gains the ability to intercept attacks aimed at Cookie as auto-crits on themselves. DM checks this trigger every scene that includes Heartstring-touched NPCs.

triggers.name: Fans Out of Control — Ember-enhanced Heartstring-touched behavior
triggers.narrative_description: Ember enhancement on top of The Great User, only active while Ember is available. Adds a stalker/assassin/guard layer keyed on gender and disposition for any Heartstring-touched humanoid in the campaign world. Disabled if Ember nullified.

triggers.name: Fans Out of Control — sleep-time ambush roll

4-6.narrative_hook: someone scoping the location, no entry

triggers.narrative_description: Cookie rolls for ambush every time she sleeps, even in safe locations, due to the stalker/assassin layer of Fans Out of Control. The secret-guard network ensures at least one nearby Heartstring-touched friendly male will appear to fight on Cookie's behalf if an ambush rolls.

triggers.name: Low-Wisdom Loss-of-Control — alcohol/drug WIS save
triggers.narrative_description: Cookie's WIS 7 cannot self-regulate. When alcohol or drugs are offered, a real WIS save fires (DC 12 baseline, scales with potency). On failure the player loses control of Cookie for the scene. Drugged or blacked-out events trigger an attempt scene; The Great User and Ember Last Stand are the safety net.

triggers.name: Halfling Luck — reroll natural 1
triggers.narrative_description: Whenever Cookie rolls a natural 1 on an attack roll, ability check, or saving throw, she rerolls and must use the new result.

triggers.name: Ember Last Stand — prone/paralyzed humanoid hijack

female.narrative_hook: mock or taunt Cookie instead of finishing her

triggers.narrative_description: When Cookie is prone or paralyzed and the Ember is active, humanoid enemies in line of sight have their kill instinct hijacked. Females mock; males attempt to mate (STR save vs Cookie's spell DC to refuse). Last-ditch biological defense — survival via degradation.

triggers.name: Dance Inspiration — +d12 on dance Performance checks
triggers.narrative_description: When Cookie incorporates dancing into a Performance check, she rolls an additional d12 and adds it. Singing/speaking-only Performance uses the standard d20 + modifier.

triggers.name: Dancer's Punishment — failed Performance = prone
triggers.narrative_description: Any failed Performance check (combat, casting, social, anything) drops Cookie prone immediately. Universal class penalty.

triggers.name: Action Exclusivity — Tai Chi and dance spells mutually exclusive

effect.narrative_hook: Cookie must choose Tai Chi OR a dance spell on a given turn, not both.

triggers.narrative_description: Tai Chi and dance spells (Healing Dance, Dispel, Planar Waltz, Dragon's Roar) cannot coexist on the same turn. Cookie picks one or the other; switching requires ending one before starting the other.

triggers.name: Spell initiation — Performance roll required for dance spells (fail = prone)
triggers.narrative_description: Healing Dance, Dance of Dispel, Planar Waltz, and Dragon's Roar all require a Performance roll to initiate; failure drops Cookie prone and fizzles the spell. Stunning Kicks (teleport-strike, not dance) and Protector's Surge (touch, bonus action) do NOT require this roll.

triggers.name: Spell concentration — dance spells use CHA via DDR
triggers.narrative_description: Concentration on dance spells routes through Charisma instead of Constitution per Dance Dance Revolution.

class_features.name: Dance Inspiration
class_features.summary: When dancing during a Performance check, roll d20 + d12 + modifier instead of d20 + modifier — movement adds magic to the roll.
class_features.mechanical_effect: Performance check while dancing: d20 + d12 + modifier. Singing/speaking-only Performance uses d20 + modifier as normal.

class_features.name: Dancer's Tai Chi
class_features.summary: DEX-based kick martial art with two performance rolls per turn — start-of-turn grants ally free attacks, end-of-turn grants 75% dodge; failures drop Cookie prone.

mechanics.attack: 1 kick per round. DEX-based (d20 + DEX mod + prof to hit). 1d6 bludgeoning. Always causes PRONE on hit.
mechanics.start_of_turn: Performance roll (d20 + d12 + mod). DC 12. SUCCESS: all allies seeing Cookie get 1 free attack this round. FAIL: Cookie falls prone and loses her turn.
mechanics.end_of_turn: Performance roll (d20 + d12 + mod). DC 12. SUCCESS: 75% dodge chance vs all enemy attacks until next turn. FAIL: Cookie falls prone.
mechanics.design_note: Most swingy combatant possible. Prone triggers Ember Last Stand.

class_features.name: Dancer's Punishment
class_features.summary: Any failed Performance check — combat, casting, social — drops Cookie prone immediately.
class_features.mechanical_effect: Universal class-wide penalty. Failed Performance check (any context) = Cookie falls prone. Applies to Tai Chi rolls, spell initiation rolls, social performance rolls, etc.

class_features.name: Action Exclusivity: Tai Chi vs Dance Spells
class_features.summary: Tai Chi kicks and dance spells (Healing Dance, Dispel, Planar Waltz, Dragon's Roar) are mutually exclusive on a given turn — pick one.
class_features.mechanical_effect: Cookie may use Tai Chi OR a dance spell on a given turn, never both. Switching between them requires ending one before starting the other (e.g., stopping Tai Chi to begin a multi-round dance spell).

class_features.name: Performance Domain Bonus (Performer Archetype)
class_features.summary: Each successful Performance/Persuasion roll grants 25% of the level-gap as XP — Cookie levels through using her voice and her dance, not just kills.
class_features.mechanical_effect: Performer support archetype. Any successful Performance, Persuasion, or domain-tagged check awards 25% of the current level→next-level XP gap as bonus XP. Stacks with combat XP and quest XP. Performer-specific — does not apply to other support archetypes (healer, scholar, infiltrator, crafter, diplomat).
class_features.design_note: Encourages Cookie to lean into performance and social play; her primary XP loop is the stage, not the dungeon.

class_features.name: Party-Size XP Penalty
class_features.summary: Combat XP is divided by full party size (5+ Wardbreakers = combat XP / 5) — support classes scale by skill not by attendance.
class_features.mechanical_effect: Combat XP for monsters defeated is divided by total party size. Cookie alone = ×4 multiplier (solo). Party of 2 = ×2. Party of 3 = ×1. Party of 4 = ×0.75. Party of 5+ = ×0.6 (support divisor). Stacks with the engine's EXP_MULTIPLIERS table; the divisor is automatic when party_size is set on the engine.
class_features.design_note: Cookie's primary XP source is performance, not combat. The party-size penalty mechanically pushes her to do what her class is good at — singing, dancing, persuading — instead of trying to compete with martial classes for kill XP.

spells_known.name: Protector's Surge
spells_known.effect: +4 to all ability scores (and corresponding modifier increases). Only one creature affected at a time — casting again on a new target ends the previous.
spells_known.limitation: Only works when Cookie is in a genuinely unsafe situation and the target is actively defending her.

spells_known.name: Healing Dance
spells_known.duration: 3 rounds (concentration — CHA-based via Dance Dance Revolution)
spells_known.description: Cookie performs a gossip song praising nature and love, based on one of Ignis's famous 'Four Seasons' series. Requires Performance roll to initiate — if fail, Cookie falls prone and the spell fails.
spells_known.effect_during: 25% max HP heal per round to all allies within 30 ft for duration of performance.
spells_known.effect_on_completion: 100% max HP heal to all allies within 30 ft. All healed allies gain Regeneration buff: 10% max HP per round for 1 hour.
spells_known.failure_initiation: Performance roll fail = Cookie falls prone, spell does not begin.
spells_known.failure_concentration: If concentration is broken before round 3 completes, only the per-round 25% healing applies. No completion heal or regen buff.
spells_known.limitation: Requires dancing (triggers Dance Inspiration d12 on performance checks). Cookie cannot attack or move more than 5 ft per round while channeling.
spells_known.ember_enhanced: 10× healing values when Ember is active. Disabled if Ember nullified.

spells_known.name: Stunning Kicks
spells_known.range: 60 ft (teleport to each enemy, return to original position)
spells_known.duration: Instant (stun lasts 2 rounds guaranteed, STR/CON save to escape starting round 3)
spells_known.description: Cookie teleports to each enemy within 60 ft and attempts a kick before returning to her original position. Each kick requires a DEX-based attack roll (d20 + DEX mod + prof). On hit: target is stunned for 2 rounds (no save), must make STR or CON save to escape starting on round 3.
spells_known.attack_roll: d20 + DEX mod + prof per target
spells_known.on_hit: 1d6 bludgeoning + stun 2 rounds (guaranteed), STR/CON save to escape round 3+
spells_known.ember_enhanced: Disabled if Ember nullified (teleport component requires Ember).

spells_known.name: Dance of Dispel
spells_known.duration: 2 rounds (concentration — CHA-based via Dance Dance Revolution)
spells_known.description: Requires Performance roll to initiate — if fail, Cookie falls prone. Cookie dances for 2 rounds with concentration. On successful completion: clears ALL buffs and Ember effects on the battlefield that do not originate from Cookie, within 120 ft. Only takes effect after successfully maintaining concentration for full 2 rounds.
spells_known.failure_initiation: Performance roll fail = Cookie falls prone, spell does not begin.
spells_known.failure_concentration: If concentration breaks before round 2, no effect.

spells_known.name: Planar Waltz
spells_known.duration: 2 rounds casting, buff/debuff lasts until end of combat
spells_known.description: Requires Performance roll to initiate — if fail, Cookie falls prone. Cookie dances for 2 rounds with concentration. On successful completion: all enemies are locked to the physical plane (cannot go ethereal/phase). All allies gain ability to go ethereal as an extra action and deal +5 spirit damage (+50 if Ember active). Any undead, spirit, demon, or extradimensional enemy that dies during this dance suffers true death (cannot be resurrected/reformed). Effects persist until end of combat.
spells_known.failure_initiation: Performance roll fail = Cookie falls prone, spell does not begin.
spells_known.failure_concentration: If concentration breaks before round 2, no effect.
spells_known.ember_enhanced: Spirit damage increases from +5 to +50 when Ember active. Disabled if Ember nullified.

spells_known.name: Dragon's Roar Dance
spells_known.duration: 2 rounds casting, buff lasts until end of combat
spells_known.description: Requires Performance roll to initiate — if fail, Cookie falls prone. Cookie picks one element from a colored dragon: fire, acid, ice, water, or lightning. She dances for 2 rounds channeling that dragon. On successful completion: all allies gain +10 damage of chosen element type (+100 if Ember active) until end of combat. Only takes effect after successfully maintaining concentration for full 2 rounds.
spells_known.failure_initiation: Performance roll fail = Cookie falls prone, spell does not begin.
spells_known.failure_concentration: If concentration breaks before round 2, no effect.

_chapter_log_from_tracker.title: First Night in Varenholm
_chapter_log_from_tracker.summary: Arrived, enrolled Academy, V.E.A. stamp, bought outfits, met Torren at Gilt Lily (failed WIS by 9). 3,375 XP. Level 1→2.

_chapter_log_from_tracker.title: The Indigo Hustle
_chapter_log_from_tracker.summary: Maeven fitting, Torren seduction, Fern hired, guild rally, bandit camp raid (Tai Chi debut, 6 combat Performance rolls). 20,000 XP. Level 2→4.

_chapter_log_from_tracker.title: Golden Returns
_chapter_log_from_tracker.summary: Survey Stones quest with party. Castor caught, Edric paid 6 GP (Pretty Privilege). Earring deal at Pale Lantern with Isolde. 6,000 XP. Level 4→5.

_chapter_log_from_tracker.title: The Bat Dance
_chapter_log_from_tracker.summary: First Academy class — Dragon's Fire dance (22 vs DC 14, Heartstring shifted 9/15 students). Picked up outfits. Chose Academy fieldwork bounty over combat quests. Joined Team Two (Silka, Ivor, Fenella). Invented echolocation tap-dance at Burnt Amphitheatre (Performance 38, max d12) — mapped underchambers without entering. Detected phase spiders. Combat: 3 Phase Spiders at Burnt Amphitheatre, all FATALITY by Fenella. Healing Dance completed (100% regen buff active ~1 hr post-combat). Earned 99 GP (50 spider parts + 49 quest bonus). Purchased Healer's Ring + Anklet of Unarmed Combat from enchanter (40 GP). DEX 3→7, AC 18→22, kick now 6d8+7. 6,673 XP. Level 5.

_chapter_log_from_tracker.title: The Four Seasons of Love
_chapter_log_from_tracker.summary: Bridge troll quest (ogre seduction via Performance nat 20 + Heartstring). Starling debut: Four Seasons of Love — 4 songs, 4 outfits, standing ovation, 200+ crowd. LOCAL SENSATION fame. Two level-ups (5→7). 30,750 XP.

_chapter_log_from_tracker.title: Gold in the Morning
_chapter_log_from_tracker.summary: LOCAL SENSATION fame. Ashworth's 2nd class — Kettlebrook lullaby (Performance 32 vs DC 16). Negotiated accelerated graduation: 3 Persuasion checks (DC 20 success, DC 18 fail, DC 22 nat 20). Starling accepted as Term 1 recital. Term 2 fieldwork assigned: Halbert's funeral (Day 7). Visited widow Maret. Bought mourning dress. 21,500 XP. Level 7→8.

_chapter_log_from_tracker.title: Daddy's Girl
_chapter_log_from_tracker.summary: Halbert's funeral (Performance 23 vs DC 14, vocal only). Guild registration updated to L8, hire rate 5 GP. Wardbreakers encounter — Senna recognized Ember. Cookie claimed Dragon Emperor as father. Cliffhanger. 7,250 XP. Level 8.

_chapter_log_from_tracker.title: Six Gold and a Stew
_chapter_log_from_tracker.summary: Senna's reply at the bar; Cookie publicly outed herself as Ankuspawn and dodged the paternity question. Cult-of-Anku scrivener filed her name. Buzzed on honey mead (failed WIS DC 12). Declined Selwyn+Beldra 2-day contract and Jareth's 12-GP Calverton lead. Took the Gilded Thread cellar wererat job with Brass Whistle silver-tier crew (Falconer/Tova/Mograth) for a 1.5 GP split. 8 wererats cleared in ~30 seconds: Stunning Kicks teleport opener, 2 Tai Chi FATALITIES (bludgeoning paste under new rule), Falconer 2 nat-20 crits. Hazel paid + threw in lifetime 10% retail discount + voluntary upper-tier women's circuit promotion. Walked home, ate Fern's stew, slept by 21:00, NAT 20 ambush check = quiet night. Long rest completed. Day 8 dawn 05:30. 36,220 XP earned (4 Performance domain bonuses + combat XP). Level 8→10 (starter cap reached). Ember Enhancement at growth ceiling — story-trigger gate now in effect.

_chapter_log_from_tracker.title: Daughters
_chapter_log_from_tracker.summary: Day 8 — Academy class with Ashworth: longing-emotion projection (Performance 28 vs DC 14 success), Heartstring landed on all 12 students, Silka visibly affected. After class: Ashworth closed Term 2 fieldwork (Halbert satisfactory), assigned final fieldwork (Sunken Playhouse 2-day expedition + Civic Auditorium recital for graduation). Cookie accepted, signed parchment. Caught Silka in corridor, invited her to the fieldwork — Silka accepted (under Lyssa's standing instruction + un-named sister-resonance pull). At the Adventurer's Guild: hired the Wardbreakers (Senna's Bronze rate gift — 12 GP for 3 days + paternity-question term to be answered before return). Prep run: Bursar redemption (15 GP), Gilded Thread (NEW green outfit purchased 6.3 SP after Hazel discount), Pale Lantern (Isolde threw in dried Ember-reactive herb 'for the ruin'), General store, Millward Chandlery (Fern goodbye). South gate departure 13:00, six-person party (Cookie + Silka + Senna + Finch + Varn + Thessaly). Marshmouth Inn arrival sunset, camp in inn-yard. Senna deferred the paternity question to the trip ('your head on the work, not on me'). Cookie's WIS-7 stunt at 00:15 — pressed against Finch in his bedroll, moaning, milk-flask prank at 00:25 (Performance 19 vs DC 18 by 1 to sell as halfling clown-pose vs assault tableau). Senna walked over at 00:30, read it as the stunt it was, laid the heat on Finch as Wardbreaker-discipline failure, sent Cookie back to her own bedroll. Finch wears the heat for the night. Silka's sister-resonance pain compounded silently. NAT 20 ambush check — quiet night. Long rest completed. Day 9 dawn 06:30, breakfast in heavy silence. Marsh-road approach. At ~09:30, two hours in, Thessaly's crystal lit continuous gold. Proscenium peak visible 200 yards south. Silver-paste reapplied. Cookie chose the west causeway approach (fast, expected). 100 ft in, around a stone pillar — flute, drum, woman's voice. Silka knew the song; her face went wax-pale, hand to silver chain. Thessaly's crystal red-shot: 'Charm-resonant. Active. Bardic, College of Glamour signature.' Frogs went silent. Cookie's Heartstring pushed forward; something pushed back. Chapter ends on cliffhanger: party paused on the causeway, music forming syllables, Senna's hold-fist up, Cookie's call pending. **Lyssa Vane Phase 4 venue first defensive layer engaged — combat or parley imminent.**
_chapter_log_from_tracker.rolls: Performance DC 14 (28, success +14 — class longing-projection); Insight DC 13 (12, fail by 1 — could not read Silka's conflict); Crowd assist DC 13 (19, success — Wardbreakers accept Bronze-rate); WIS save (Cookie didn't drink yesterday's mead — N/A); Cookie Stealth DC 14 (15, scrape success — cross camp past Thessaly); Finch WIS save vs Heartstring DC 12 (3, FAIL by 9 — held by Great User only); Senna passive register (23 — fully awake); Performance DC 18 (19 by 1 — sell stunt as clown-pose vs assault read).

_chapter_log_from_tracker.title: The Reader
_chapter_log_from_tracker.summary: Day 9 09:30 — Sunken Playhouse causeway, ~100 ft from proscenium pillar. Frogs went silent. Senna held the line; Varn anchored greatshield; Finch ghosted right with bow nocked but uncomedown. Cookie chose to push forward at speed — twenty-second sprint to the pillar. Eira surrendered preemptively at the dry side of the column with two charmed marsh-village civilians behind her, hands raised, ninety-second negotiation request. Ground beneath the pillar was scattered with the silver chain-fragments Cookie had shattered off Silka's collarbones at Halbert's funeral — someone (Eira) had carried the pieces to the playhouse. Cookie called for delegation: Varn brought the civilians out of charm with patient Combat-Magnetism touch (both villagers eating dried apple and answering to their own names by the eastern crossroads); Finch produced a Guild-issue deposition slip and supervised Eira's writing under graphite-stick threat. Eira's testimony: Lyssa called the patron 'the Reader,' never saw his face, dispatches arrived monthly via a Stormhaven tea-merchant courier who didn't know he was a courier, drops went to a dead-letter box behind the Pale Lantern's loading dock (NOT Isolde), the Reader paid 5 SP per gold-eyed name + 10 SP for confirmed Ember activity, never asked about Silka, never named Eira, two days ago received a halfling-Kettlebrook name in his last letter and watched Lyssa turn the wrong color reading it. Eira signed and dated the deposition. Senna walked Cookie five paces away from witnesses to the marsh-edge and asked the formal contract-terms paternity question: Kenji, the man the Wardbreakers worked under in the Vyranth campaign, the one who held the Sundered Gate, alive — yes or no. Cookie answered. Senna refiled the relationship: Bronze contract closes on this trip, Wardbreaker dependent status begins (paperwork pending in Varenholm). Return trip compressed thirty hours into montage — Senna refused to camp on hot evidence, six souls reached east gate Day 10 dusk. Adventurer's Guild handoff: Gruff stamped contract complete + chain of custody + prisoner intake, paid Bronze rate + 5 GP completion bonus, signed Academy capstone copy of the deposition for Ashworth's file, pushed Cookie to Day 11 morning debrief. Day 11 08:00 — Foundations of Performance lecture: two hours, Cookie sat third row, Silka second; Silka's iris flicked gold for half a second when Cookie walked in (chain-collapse symptoms now externally visible), her hand reflexively went to a collarbone with no chain on it anymore. Crown Quarter merchant's daughter watched both of them with the wrong kind of disinterest (Lyssa's network still operational at minimum). Post-class: Ashworth retained Cookie + Silka by surname (revealing Silka's family name as Mireille), reviewed the deposition, asked whether Cookie wrote the structural appendix herself en route, stamped Academy review / capstone accepted / course honors on the cover sheet, and slid the folder back to Cookie. Recital booked for Day 18. Off the record: Ashworth ordered Cookie to leave the Reader's name alone for ten days. Then Ashworth turned to Silka and put her on partial sabbatical — performance theory only, no field work, no public performances, daily check-in at the office, framed as 'you are not in trouble, you are changing, I would prefer to be the person watching when it happens.' Silka accepted. Outside Ashworth's office, Silka stopped walking three feet from Cookie with gold ink rising into her visible skin and no one yet having told her what she is. CHAPTER CLOSE.
_chapter_log_from_tracker.rolls: No combat. No skill checks called for in narration. The mission was procedural — diplomatic resolution of Eira; deposition extraction by Finch's intimidation framework; capstone assessment review by Ashworth (no DC, just professional sign-off). The paternity exchange was a contract-terms verbal confirmation, not a roll.

_chapter_log_from_tracker.title: From Kettlebrook
_chapter_log_from_tracker.summary: Day 12 — Crown Quarter regular attempted to walk Fern home from the Millward courtyard with charm-tagged warmth; Fern recognized the wrongness in three sentences, yelled for Mrs. Millward and the Millward stable boys, attacker left without confirming the address. Cookie sent courier-pigeons same evening: Tomas Wren in Kettlebrook (pulled Daisy into inn back-rooms for four days under 'spring damp' cover), Hazel's noble-women circuit (watch on Daisy's road). Lyssa retaliation STALLED — her people reported back that Cookie's network was active. Day 14 — Ashworth offered three recital pieces; Cookie rejected all three and wrote her own original, titled 'From Kettlebrook,' refused to play any of it for Ashworth before performance night. Silka attended every afternoon practice on her sabbatical, sat against the practice-room door, hands shaking less each day, gold rising visibly above her collar by Day 17. Day 18 — Civic Auditorium recital, three-tier seating, line around the block. Council bench full (Vess in row 2). Bardic College of Ashenmere sent their Master of Voice. Torren in row 11. Fern + Millward stable boys in standing-room balcony. Senna/Finch/Varn in row 3 in formal Wardbreaker colors. Lyssa's surveillance asset (Crown Quarter merchant's daughter) seated row 22. Cookie performed solo, unaccompanied open, halfling field-tune melody from age six. Verse 1: Kettlebrook origin. Verse 2: Varenholm rise (Anklet filigree first lit on heel-stop, gold to front 3 rows). Bridge: Sunken Playhouse without naming it (chain that wasn't supposed to break, sister who was someone else's plan — Silka stopped breathing in the wing). Final chorus: world-scale ambition, Stormhaven through Sundered Gate, Dragon Emperor, half-brother Ignis. Full Ember resonance bloom on last 12 bars. Performance roll d20+9 ADV (14/14 → 14) + d12(5) = 28 vs DC 22 (margin +6). 240-person standing ovation. Vess + Bardic Master + Ashworth all stood. Backstage: Ashworth signed gold-thread parchment — Master of Voice credential, touring eligibility unrestricted, Ashenmere guest residency offered. Off-record: Ashworth said in 31 years she had not seen what she saw, and reminded Cookie 'whoever your father is, you earned every step of what just happened, and none of it came from him.' Hallway after: Fern collision-hug, Senna nod, Varn shoulder-drop, Finch tired-and-proud. Torren sobbing in a doorway. Hazel + 3 unnamed noblewomen forming the next business-layer (tour bookings, contracts). Silka waiting in the stagedoor wing for the entire backstage scrum, gold visible, hands folded, eyes wet, not part of any other orbit. Ashworth's last gesture: chin-dip toward Silka, 'not permission but might be advice.' Outside: somewhere two streets over a tavern doorway already fragmenting the song. Chapter close — three open paths: sister-recognition scene with Silka, Vess + Ashenmere residency political-cover arc, or defy embargo and hit the Reader's Pale Lantern dropbox tonight.
