player_input.name: Cookie
player_input.class_concept: Dancer (bard/healer — buffs via dancing and singing, high charm, support)
player_input.background: Ankuspawn raised by single halfling mother in a coalition town near Varenholm. Spent youth cleaning inns. Saved 5 GP over years for bard university tuition. Mom forbids the bard life — 'no daughter of mine will grow up to be a slut.' Today is her 21st birthday. She has to break the news.
player_input.personal_goal: Become a famous bard like Ignis

narrator_style.author: Aleron Kong
narrator_style.voice: Irreverent, funny, detailed mechanics woven into prose, pop culture nods, narrator has personality and attitude. The narrator grins while writing fight scenes. Stat details land naturally in the flow — not dry readouts but part of the fun. Humor even in tense moments.

appearance.height_build: 4'10", athletic dancer's build — curvy but toned from years of dancing, compact halfling frame with surprising flexibility
appearance.hair: Bright blue, worn in twin tails — bouncy and expressive, moves with her when she dances
appearance.eyes: Gold-flecked (Ankuspawn trait) — warm golden glow against dark skin, catches firelight
appearance.distinguishing_marks: Faint golden scroll-like markings on shoulders and torso — she thinks they're birthmarks, actually Ember traces from her Ankuspawn heritage. Pointed ears (halfling, slightly elongated from Ankuspawn blood). Small gold hoop earrings.
appearance.style_vibe: Bold and flashy — favors deep blues, reds, and golds. Dancer's clothes: flowing skirts, lace trim, ribbon accents, bare feet or light dancing shoes. Moves like she's always mid-performance. Confident smile that makes people underestimate how sharp she is. The kind of presence that fills a room the moment she walks in.
appearance.fashion_quirk: THE FIREBIRD LOOK — Cookie is an obsessive Ignis fangirl and dresses accordingly. Ignis, being a fire dragon in human form, normalized wearing barely-there outfits in public — loose wraps, open corsets, sheer fabrics, lingerie-as-outerwear — because dragons genuinely do not understand human clothing modesty. Ignis made it fashion. A generation of young performers adopted 'The Firebird Look': sexy, flowing, almost-lingerie dancer outfits worn as daily clothes. Cookie has been dressing this way since she was old enough to fight Daisy about it. DM: randomize Cookie's outfit each day — always Ignis-inspired (reds, golds, sheer/lace, skin-showing, dancer-functional), always a new look, always barefoot or in light dance shoes. Cookie changes outfits daily as part of her identity — fashion IS performance for her.
appearance.fashion_spending_mechanic: COMPULSIVE SHOPPER (WIS -2 consequence) — Cookie cannot resist buying new outfits. DM: when randomizing each day's outfit, note where she bought it (Kettlebrook hometown shops, Varenholm boutiques, market stalls, or later — wherever she travels). Auto-deduct cost from her gold/silver/copper. Most outfits are worn once then added to her growing wardrobe. This is a passive gold drain the player must actively manage — Pretty Privilege (10x income from male employers) generates cash, but Cookie's shopping habit burns through it. The player will eventually notice their money shrinking and need to find a workaround. Price outfits at real-world equivalent per economy rules (cheap market find = 5-15 CP, boutique piece = 3-8 SP, luxury item = 1+ GP). Track wardrobe count in inventory.
wardrobe: First Flame — crimson Firebird wrap, sheer silk cross-chest tie-at-hip, gold thread hems, black lace bandeau (Varenholm, Maeven's shop, Day 4 morning)
Saffron gold Firebird halter wrap — cropped, bare midriff, matching fingerless gloves, thigh-high amber boots, black choker (Day 6 outfit)
Dark grey mourning dress — long sleeves, high neck, simple hem (canal district tailor, Day 6, 5 CP)

player_knowledge: Cookie knows she has gold-flecked eyes. Her mom never explained why.
Cookie knows the Dragon Emperor exists — everyone does. She does not know he is her father.
Cookie knows Ignis as a famous bard — 'The Firebird' — who performs kingdom-wide with magical fire shows. Ignis is her idol and the reason she wants to be a bard.
Cookie has heard of the Ashenveil Academy — a prestigious school in the southeast. She does NOT know about the Cult of Anku or Ankuspawn harvesting.
Cookie has felt strange things — emotions from strangers, a warmth in her chest when she dances, men staring too long. She thinks she's just pretty. She doesn't know it's an aura.
Cookie's mom never talks about her father. 'He was nobody. Forget it.'
dm_only_crossover_hooks: Cookie's mother met Kenji at an inn 22 years ago. He was passing through — Bane of Eve encounter. Cookie's mom never learned his real name.
The Cult of Anku has agents in Varenholm looking for Ankuspawn. A female Ankuspawn with gold eyes enrolling in bard school will eventually appear on their radar.
Ignis occasionally performs at the Varenholm Academy during festival season. Cookie could meet her idol — and not know they share a father.
Vess (Kenji's former companion) lives in the Varenholm area and is desperate for Nyx's fertility elixir. She might cross paths with Cookie without either knowing the connection.
inherited_lore.lore_seed: [Cult of Anku — Ankuspawn as targets] A young woman with gold eyes disappeared from the next town over three months ago. Her mother received a letter saying she'd been 'accepted to a special program at the Ashenveil.' She never wrote again. (Connects to: Nyx's Ankuspawn harvesting operation — Cookie is exactly the kind of person they're looking for.)

locations._rule: Every new character adds exactly 3 locations to the world map.

home_base.name: Kettlebrook
home_base.from_worlds_middle: Heartland — institutional core, kingdom infrastructure everywhere
home_base.terrain_and_vibe: A modest coalition town a half-day's walk from Varenholm. Brick row houses, three inns, a small market square, a temple to the common gods. Halfling and human population mixed. Working-class — the people who wash the sheets and shoe the horses for the capital.
home_base.travel_time_to_major_hub: Half a day on foot to Varenholm, 2 hours by cart
home_base.connection_to_character: Cookie grew up here. She and her mother Daisy clean rooms at The Wren's Rest inn. Cookie knows every floorboard, every regular, every shortcut through the back alleys. This is the place she loves and needs to leave.
home_base.registry_entry: Kettlebrook — working-class coalition town near Varenholm, halfling/human mixed population. Cookie's hometown.

adventure_site.name: The Sunken Playhouse
adventure_site.distance_from_home_base: 2 days travel (between Kettlebrook and Varenholm, off the main road)
adventure_site.terrain_and_vibe: An ancient outdoor amphitheater that sank into the earth during a ley-line shift decades ago. Half-buried stone seats, a cracked stage, acoustics that still carry whispers across the ruin. Now occupied by Lyssa and her troupe — a rival performance company that uses charm magic, theft, and intimidation to eliminate competition.
adventure_site.why_dangerous: Base of operations for Lyssa Vane, a charismatic bard-enchantress who controls the regional performance circuit through blackmail, sabotage, and magical coercion. Anyone who threatens her monopoly on heartland entertainment gets silenced.
adventure_site.registry_entry: The Sunken Playhouse — collapsed pre-kingdom amphitheater between Kettlebrook and Varenholm. Occupied by a rogue bard troupe.

discovery_location.name: The Resonance Chamber
discovery_location.connection_to_existing_lore: Pre-kingdom ley-line infrastructure. The chamber sits on a junction where the amphitheater's acoustics connect to a deeper network — a place where sound becomes magic. Ancient bards used it. It also resonates with Ember energy, which is why Cookie feels drawn to it. A young dragon (connected to the dragon treaty edge case) has been nesting here, drawn by the same resonance.
discovery_location.what_the_player_learns_here: Cookie discovers that her dancing and singing produce visible magical effects in this chamber — golden light, warmth, healing. She realizes her talent isn't just talent. Something inside her is different. The chamber's carvings reference 'the children of fire' — an old term that predates the kingdom. A young dragon sheltering here recognizes Cookie's Ember spark and reacts to her as kin. Cookie begins to understand she's connected to something much bigger than bard school.
key_npcs_here: Young bronze dragon (unnamed — treaty edge case)
Echo (a spirit/memory trapped in the chamber's acoustics)
discovery_location.registry_entry: The Resonance Chamber — hidden ley-line acoustic junction beneath the Sunken Playhouse. Pre-kingdom bard infrastructure, Ember-resonant.

ember_inheritance._rule: Ankuspawn only. Generated from class_concept + personal_goal. Player does NOT choose. Theme-locked. See ankuspawn_race.md § Ember Inheritance.
ember_inheritance.theme_description: Cookie's Ember shaped itself around her lifelong love of dance and music — the way she hums while scrubbing floors, the way her body moves to rhythms only she can hear, the way she's always wanted to make people FEEL something. The Ember amplifies voice, sound, emotional projection, and performance magic. It is who she was always going to be, just... more.

passive.name: Heartstring
passive.description: Cookie's voice and movements carry emotional undertones that bypass rational thought. When she speaks, sings, or dances, listeners feel what she feels — joy makes them smile involuntarily, sorrow brings tears, excitement raises pulses. She cannot turn this off. It's why tavern patrons always tipped her mother extra when Cookie was cleaning nearby, humming to herself. It's also why men stare — the Ankuspawn beauty aura PLUS emotional bleed is devastating.
passive.mechanical_effect: +2 to all Charisma-based checks (Persuasion, Performance, Deception, Intimidation) when speaking or performing. Listeners within 30 ft must make WIS save DC 12 or have their emotional state shifted one step toward Cookie's current emotion. Passive — always active, cannot be suppressed.

active.name: Chorus of One
active.description: Cookie can project her voice as if an entire troupe were performing — harmonies, countermelodies, percussion rhythms, all from her solo voice. The sound fills a space unnaturally, resonating off walls and through bodies. She can target the resonance to bolster allies (healing warmth, courage, pain suppression) or disorient enemies (dissonant frequencies, nausea, vertigo). She discovered this on her 18th birthday when she sang happy birthday to herself in her room and the entire inn heard a full choir.
active.mechanical_effect: Bonus action. 60 ft range. Choose one: (A) BOLSTER — up to 3 allies gain 1d8+CHA temp HP and advantage on next saving throw; or (B) DISORIENT — up to 3 enemies make CON save DC 13 or suffer disadvantage on attacks for 1 round and lose 10 ft movement. Uses per day: 3 (scales to 5 at level 6, 7 at level 10).

surge.name: The Golden Note
surge.description: Cookie opens her mouth and sings a single, impossibly pure note — a sound that doesn't belong in the mortal world. Gold light pulses from her throat and eyes. The note resonates with every living thing in range, forcing a moment of absolute emotional truth. Allies feel unshakeable courage and clarity. Enemies feel their deepest fears and guilts surface. Objects vibrate. Glass shatters. The air itself hums for minutes afterward. This is a fragment of what Kenji's Ember can do at full power — projected through a dancer's voice instead of a warrior's blade. It leaves Cookie shaking, voiceless, and exhausted afterward.
surge.mechanical_effect: Action. 120 ft radius. ALL allies: healed 3d10+CHA, cured of fear/charm, gain advantage on all rolls for 1 round. ALL enemies: WIS save DC 15 or frightened for 1 minute (repeat save each turn). Enemies who fail by 5+ are also stunned for 1 round. After use: Cookie gains 1 level of exhaustion, loses her voice for 1 hour (no verbal spells or Chorus of One), and Heartstring passive is suppressed until she completes a short rest.
surge.exhaustion_cost: 1 level of exhaustion + 1 hour voiceless + Heartstring suppressed until short rest

theme_lock: This Ember ONLY enhances: voice, sound, emotional projection, crowd control, and performance magic.
CANNOT enhance physical combat — Cookie's Ember will not make her sword swing harder, her dodge faster, or her armor tougher. She has to learn those the hard way.
CANNOT grant stealth or invisibility — Resonance is about BEING NOTICED, not hiding. Cookie's Ember actively works against stealth (she literally glows when emotional).
CANNOT manipulate objects, elements, or materials — no telekinesis, no fire, no ice, no shaping stone. Sound and emotion only.
CANNOT read minds or extract specific thoughts — Heartstring reads EMOTIONS, not information. She knows someone is afraid, not WHAT they're afraid of.

CANNOT resurrect, cure disease, or perform medical healing — Chorus of One grants temporary HP (morale/adrenaline), not wound closure. The Golden Note heals through emotional catharsis, not divine magic.

nyx_harvest_value.extract_type: Emotional resonance essence — concentrated empathic projection distilled from a living Resonance Ankuspawn
nyx_harvest_value.elixir_product: Mass charm elixirs (one dose can entrance an entire banquet hall), emotional override potions (force a specific emotion for hours), and Nyx's crown jewel project: a permanent voice enchantment that makes the user's commands feel like the listener's own desires
nyx_harvest_value.target_priority: critical — female Ankuspawn + Resonance theme = highest-value combination for Nyx's charm research. Cookie is exactly what the anonymous patron paying Lyssa Vane's reports is looking for.

antagonist._rule: Scaled for levels 5-8. Regional menace, not world-ending.
antagonist.name: Lyssa Vane
antagonist.motivation: Lyssa was a talented bard who graduated from Varenholm Academy 10 years ago. She built a performance empire in the heartland through charm magic and intimidation. She controls which acts get booked, which venues stay open, and which performers get 'opportunities.' Anyone more talented than her gets absorbed into her troupe (charmed) or destroyed. Cookie — a natural talent with supernatural charisma and an Ember spark — is everything Lyssa fears: someone who could outshine her without even trying.
antagonist.relationship_to_goal: rival — Lyssa will not allow another bard to rise in the heartland without her permission. Cookie's raw talent and Ankuspawn magnetism threaten Lyssa's entire operation.

lieutenants.name: Rook
lieutenants.role: Enforcer. Former bouncer turned muscle. Handles the physical intimidation — wrecking instruments, threatening venue owners, roughing up performers who don't pay Lyssa's 'booking fee.'

lieutenants.name: Silka

relationship.last_event: Day 8 Dance of Dispel shattered her silver chain — gold eyes returning, scroll markings blooming, sister-recognition imminent.

lieutenants.race: Human Ankuspawn (half-sister to Cookie — neither knows)
lieutenants.role: Spy. An Ankuspawn performer charmed by Lyssa and planted at the Varenholm Academy as a student. Reports back to Lyssa about promising new talent — especially anyone with unusual abilities or gold eyes. Irony: Silka IS one of the gold-eyed targets she's hunting for.
lieutenants.appearance: Tall, slim, willowy. Dark hair past shoulders, straight, center-parted. Pale grey eyes — gold Ankuspawn flecks SUPPRESSED by a silver chain charm (Lyssa's gift, actually an Ember suppression amulet). Fair/pale skin. Conservative dress: high collars, long sleeves, muted greens and browns — deliberately hides golden scroll-like Ankuspawn markings on collarbones and forearms. Ink-stained right middle finger. Quiet-pretty, tries not to be noticed. The opposite of Cookie in every visual way.

ember.stage: SUPPRESSED — silver chain amulet dampens all Ember abilities. Would be Unreliable if freed.
ember.reveal_timing: SAVE FOR HIGH-DANGER MISSION — several chapters later. Chain comes off in life-or-death moment when Silka must choose between Lyssa's mission and Cookie's life. Do NOT reveal early.

tier_1_passive.name: Fade
tier_1_passive.description: Silka's presence dims. People's eyes slide past her unless actively looking. Always active when unsuppressed, cannot turn off.
tier_1_passive.mechanical_effect: +2 Stealth and Deception checks. 30 ft radius — WIS DC 14 to notice her when she doesn't want attention. Those within range find it hard to remember details about her afterward (WIS DC 12 or memories become vague).

tier_2_active.name: Phantom Echo
tier_2_active.mode_duplicate: Creates illusory copy of herself or ONE other person. Copy can move and speak short phrases. Appears solid — INT DC 13 to see through. Lasts 1 minute or until touched.
tier_2_active.mode_cloak: Extends Fade to up to 3 allies within 30 ft for 1 minute (group stealth, same WIS DC 14).

tier_3_surge.name: The Vanishing
tier_3_surge.description: 120 ft AOE. All allies: TRUE invisibility for 1 minute (sight AND sound dampened). All enemies: INT DC 15 save or confused — forget what they were fighting, who was here, what they were doing. 1 minute.
tier_3_surge.cost: 1 exhaustion + visibility LOCKS for 1 hour (hyper-visible, glowing, impossible to miss — opposite of Fade) + Fade suppressed until short rest.
tier_3_surge.design_note: Mirrors Cookie's Golden Note cost: Cookie loses her voice, Silka loses her shadow. Both sacrifice their core identity.

sibling_resonance.trigger: Two Ankuspawn within 30 ft, both Embers unsuppressed
sibling_resonance.effect: Cookie's Heartstring DCs +2, Silka's Fade DCs +2. Both feel unexplainable warmth — Embers from same source recognizing each other.
sibling_resonance.narrative_use: Unmistakable to anyone with magical training (Ashworth, Isolde). Proves shared bloodline.
sibling_resonance.status: IMPOSSIBLE while chain is on — only Cookie's side of resonance can faintly pulse

ember.dm_secret: Lyssa gave Silka the silver chain as a 'gift of trust.' It's actually an Ember suppression amulet — keeps Silka's Veil abilities and Ankuspawn markers dormant. If the chain is removed: gold eyes return, scroll markings bloom on collarbones/forearms, Fade activates uncontrollably (she starts disappearing, panicking). Lyssa knows Silka is Ankuspawn and is deliberately keeping her suppressed and controlled. The chain is Lyssa's most valuable asset — without it, Silka would realize what she is.

lieutenants.dm_secret: Silka knows she's 'different' — she had gold-flecked eyes as a child and strange markings that faded when Lyssa gave her the necklace. She does NOT know she's Ankuspawn or the Dragon Emperor's daughter. Lyssa told her the necklace 'protects her from magical detection' and that her markings were a skin condition. Silka believes this. When Cookie's Heartstring hits Silka, there's a faint Ember resonance — two Ankuspawn Embers recognizing each other. Neither girl understands what that feeling is.

lieutenants.name: Eira
lieutenants.role: Recruiter. Approaches talented performers with 'opportunities' that are actually traps — join Lyssa's troupe willingly or get charmed into it. Operates near the discovery location.

antagonist.defeat_consequences: Lyssa's charm network collapses. The charmed performers in her troupe wake up and scatter — some grateful, some traumatized. The heartland performance circuit opens up. Venues that were paying protection money are free. Cookie's path to fame is clear. But Lyssa's fall reveals she had a patron — someone was paying her to watch for gold-eyed performers and report them. The patron's identity leads to the Cult of Anku (continuation hook).
antagonist.dm_secret: Lyssa doesn't know about the Cult of Anku directly. She was contacted by an anonymous patron 2 years ago who pays her 5 SP per month to report any performers with 'unusual golden eyes or supernatural charm.' She thinks it's a noble with a fetish. It's actually a Cult of Anku recruiter building a target list.

campaign_spine._rule: Goal achievable by level 5-6. Remainder is defending it from the antagonist.

phase_1_arrival.summary: Cookie leaves Kettlebrook on her 21st birthday. Breaks the news to her mom. Travels to Varenholm. Enrolls in bard school. Discovers she has natural talent that goes beyond practice — her dances literally glow. Makes friends and enemies. First brush with Lyssa's network when a venue cancels Cookie's first student performance.
key_scenes: Scene 1: Cookie's 21st birthday morning. The Wren's Rest kitchen. She tells Daisy she's leaving. Daisy's reaction — anger, fear, tears, 'no daughter of mine.' Cookie leaves with 5 GP and a packed bag.

Scene 2: Arrival at Varenholm Academy. Registration. Cookie pays 1 GP tuition. Meets her first classmates. Realizes she's the poorest student there — everyone else has instruments, fine clothes, connections.
Scene 3: First dance class. Cookie performs and the room goes quiet. Golden light traces her movements. The instructor is stunned. Cookie doesn't understand why everyone is staring.

Scene 4: Cookie's first booked performance at a tavern gets cancelled last minute. The owner is apologetic but scared. 'Someone told me not to book new acts without clearance.' First hint of Lyssa's network.
phase_1_arrival.goal_status: visible but obstructed — Cookie has talent but the system is rigged against newcomers

phase_2_achievement.summary: Cookie learns to control her abilities. Her performances draw crowds. She earns a reputation — 'the golden dancer.' She performs at a major festival and gets noticed kingdom-wide. She's becoming what she dreamed of. Then Lyssa notices.
key_scenes: Cookie masters her first real performance — a dance that heals the audience. Word spreads.
Cookie performs at a Varenholm harvest festival. The crowd is massive. For one night, she feels like Ignis.
The next morning, her practice room at the Academy has been trashed. Instruments broken. A note: 'The heartland has enough bards.'
phase_2_achievement.goal_status: achieved — Cookie is becoming famous. Now someone wants to stop her.

phase_3_defense.summary: Lyssa escalates. Cookie's performances are sabotaged. A friend is charmed and turned against her. Cookie discovers the Sunken Playhouse and Lyssa's operation. She also finds the Resonance Chamber and begins to understand what she really is. The young dragon in the chamber recognizes her Ember spark.
key_scenes: Fern (Cookie's best friend from Kettlebrook, who followed her to Varenholm) goes missing. Cookie tracks her to Lyssa's troupe — Fern has been charmed.

Cookie finds the Resonance Chamber. Her dancing in that space produces full magical effects — healing, light, warmth. The carvings mention 'children of fire.' A young bronze dragon nesting there doesn't attack — it watches, fascinated.
Lyssa sends Rook to deliver a final warning. Cookie has to fight or talk her way out. Either way, she realizes Lyssa won't stop until one of them is gone.
phase_3_defense.goal_status: threatened — Cookie's reputation, friends, and safety are all under attack

phase_4_confrontation.summary: Cookie assaults the Sunken Playhouse to free the charmed performers and end Lyssa's monopoly. The final confrontation is a performance duel — bard vs. bard, charm vs. genuine talent. Cookie wins, frees the troupe, and secures her place in the heartland music scene. The Resonance Chamber reveals its deeper secret — and Lyssa's anonymous patron points toward a much larger threat.
key_scenes: Cookie enters the Sunken Playhouse. The acoustics amplify everything — this is Lyssa's home turf. Rook and the charmed troupe stand between Cookie and Lyssa.
The duel: Lyssa uses Glamour magic to dominate the stage. Cookie uses her Ember-powered dancing — genuine, raw, golden. The charmed performers start to wake up.

Lyssa falls. The charm breaks. Fern is free. The Sunken Playhouse is reclaimed. Cookie finds Lyssa's ledger — payments from an anonymous patron asking about 'gold-eyed performers.' The Cult of Anku thread opens.

Epilogue: Cookie performs at the reclaimed Playhouse. The bronze dragon from the Resonance Chamber watches from the treeline. Cookie doesn't know what she is yet — but she knows she's more than just a dancer.
phase_4_confrontation.goal_status: secured — Cookie is a famous bard. The road ahead leads to bigger questions.

main_cast.name: Daisy
main_cast.want: Wants Cookie safe, close, and away from anything that draws attention. Terrified of what Cookie is without understanding it.
main_cast.voice_hook: Talks in clipped sentences when angry, long rambling ones when worried. Calls Cookie 'little bean' when she forgets to be mad.
main_cast.lore_connection: Met Kenji once, 22 years ago, at an inn she was cleaning. He was passing through. She never learned his real name. She knows Cookie's father was 'someone important' because of the way her body changed after — she can't have more children and doesn't know why. She blames herself, not him.

relationship.last_event: Mother — daughter; Kettlebrook home; not yet told about the Ankuspawn truth.

main_cast.name: Tomas Wren
main_cast.want: Wants his inn to stay profitable and his employees to stop causing drama. Secretly proud of Cookie and slips her performance tips when Daisy isn't looking.
main_cast.voice_hook: Dry, understated. 'That's nice, dear. Now mop the landing.' But his eyes crinkle when Cookie dances.
main_cast.lore_connection: Served in the coalition army 20 years ago. Saw Varenholm during the founding. Retired to run an inn. Knows more about the world than he lets on. Has seen gold eyes before — on a traveler, years ago — and said nothing.

relationship.last_event: Innkeeper-mentor in Kettlebrook; Cookie's pre-Varenholm anchor.

main_cast.name: Fern
main_cast.location: Millward Chandlery, Varenholm (sharing room with Cookie)
main_cast.want: Wants adventure. Wants to be Cookie's best friend forever. Wants to not be boring. Will do reckless things for fun.
main_cast.voice_hook: Talks too fast, laughs too loud, asks questions she shouldn't. 'Wait wait wait — you're telling me that guy just GAVE you flowers? For dancing? Can you teach me that?'
main_cast.lore_connection: Human girl from Kettlebrook. No special heritage. Just Cookie's ride-or-die since they were kids. Gets charmed by Lyssa in Phase 3 — the emotional core of the campaign.

relationship.last_event: Best friend / roommate; helped pack for Sunken Playhouse Day 9; sworn to write.

main_cast.name: Professor Cadence Ashworth
main_cast.want: Wants to understand Cookie's abilities. Has never seen a student produce visible magic through dance alone without formal training. Suspects Cookie is 'touched by something' but doesn't know what.
main_cast.voice_hook: Measured, precise. Every word chosen carefully. 'Again. Slower. Watch where the light starts — it's not in your hands, it's in your spine. Do you feel that?'
main_cast.lore_connection: Studied at the Academy 30 years ago. Overlapped briefly with early coalition era. Has heard rumors of 'the Emperor's children' but considers it tavern gossip. Will revise that opinion when Cookie's abilities develop.

relationship.last_event: Approved Sunken Playhouse capstone; suspects Ember without naming it.

main_cast.name: Lyssa Vane
main_cast.want: Total control of heartland entertainment. No one shines brighter than her. She worked for everything she has and she'll burn anyone who threatens it — especially some barefoot halfling from Kettlebrook who glows when she dances.
main_cast.voice_hook: Silky, warm, complimentary — until the mask drops. 'You're so talented, darling. It would be such a shame if something happened to that voice.' Switches from charm to venom mid-sentence.
main_cast.lore_connection: Graduated Varenholm Academy. Built her empire legitimately at first, then turned to Glamour magic when competition got fierce. Has an anonymous patron paying her to report gold-eyed performers — doesn't know it's the Cult of Anku.

relationship.last_event: Day 8 chain shatter on Silka — control collapsing, retaliation imminent.

npcs.name: Torren
npcs.job_excuse: Textile merchant — moves cloth, linens, silks, dyes between Varenholm and coast. Recently divorced, wife in Ashenmere. Staying at Gilt Lily suite (Room 4). Tips well.

relationship.last_event: Heartstring full-fail Day 1; promoter for Starling show; hired Fern at 10x salary; will do anything Cookie asks.

npcs.name: Gilt Lily barkeep
npcs.job_excuse: Stocky woman with cropped hair. Runs the Gilt Lily bar. Gave Cookie intel on Torren unprompted ('Room 4, tips well, not the worst').

npcs.name: Maeven
npcs.job_excuse: Tailor on Chandler's Row. Owns a fitting shop. Did Cookie's indigo dress fitting. Professional, steady hands.
npcs.relationship: Business contact — fitted Cookie's indigo dress. Witnessed Cookie's dressing room exit.
npcs.met_at: Maeven's shop, Chandler's Row, Day 2 morning

npcs.name: Gruff
npcs.job_excuse: Adventurer's Guild clerk. Handles quest postings and party registration.

npcs.name: Dorith
npcs.job_excuse: Adventurer — healer. Was in Cookie's bandit camp raid party. Originally named Wynn, renamed to avoid collision with Kenji Book 4 herbalist.

npcs.name: Dalla
npcs.job_excuse: Adventurer — fighter. Bandit camp raid party member.

npcs.name: Silas
npcs.job_excuse: Adventurer — rogue. Bandit camp raid party member.

npcs.name: Marta
npcs.job_excuse: Adventurer — ranger. Bandit camp raid party member.

npcs.name: Garron
npcs.job_excuse: Adventurer — crossbowman. Bandit camp raid party member. Originally named Fen, renamed to avoid confusion with Fern (main cast).

npcs.name: Alderman Edric Tosse
npcs.job_excuse: City official — alderman. Posted the Missing Survey Stones quest at the Adventurer's Guild. Male humanoid (Pretty Privilege applied — 6 SP bounty became 6 GP).
npcs.met_at: Via Adventurer's Guild quest board, Varenholm, Day 3

npcs.name: Castor
npcs.job_excuse: Turnip farmer outside Varenholm. Stole 3 survey stones for a boundary wall after losing land to a resurvey. Caught by Cookie's party. Fined 2 GP + 30 days labor.

npcs.name: Isolde
npcs.job_excuse: Enchantress — owns The Pale Lantern in the Artisan's Quarter, Varenholm. Apprenticed under Maren Holt (The Gilt Lens). Female NPC — immune to Pretty Privilege. Aware of Heartstring aura but not swayed (enchantment experience). Struck advertising deal with Cookie: custom +2 CHA earrings (85 GP value) for 4 GP, in exchange for Cookie wearing them at The Starling show.
npcs.met_at: The Pale Lantern, Artisan's Quarter, Varenholm, Day 3

npcs.name: Maret
npcs.job_excuse: Halbert's widow. Lives above the chandler's shop on Brewer's Row, canal district. Mid-thirties, two children (boy ~8, girl Lena ~5-6). Requested Academy student musician for husband's funeral procession.
npcs.met_at: Brewer's Row apartment, canal district, Varenholm, Day 6

npcs.name: Halbert
npcs.job_excuse: Deceased dockworker, cargo crane operator. Fell from crane 6 days ago. Age 38. Worked docks since 16. Sang badly while cooking. Favorite color: river green. Cookie performing at his funeral procession (Day 7).

npcs.name: Senna Dawnmere
npcs.job_excuse: CROSS-CAMPAIGN NPC (Kenji Books 2-4). Diamond-tier Vanguard Hall (promoted after Iron Crown War alongside Kenji). Fighter / Azarinth Healer. Former Wardbreakers squad leader — hung up the name post-Book 3, took teaching post at Aldwin's War College. Chronologically late 40s but Azarinth regeneration halts aging — looks mid-20s, no scars, no grey, flawless skin. Lean, auburn braid, green fighter's eyes. The uncanny youth on a woman with decades of combat experience is deeply unsettling to anyone who knows what they're looking at. INT 18, LOW WIS (vulnerable to Heartstring DC 12), LOW CHA (blunt, says what she thinks). 25 years of encounter scaling = absurdly powerful. Semi-retired but still spars with Kenji every morning when he's around. Recognized Cookie's Ember — spent decades around AnkuNyx/Kenji. In Varenholm — possibly on personal business or pulled out of retirement.
npcs.met_at: Adventurer's Guild, Varenholm, Day 7 — walked in while Cookie was updating registration

npcs.name: Finch
npcs.job_excuse: CROSS-CAMPAIGN NPC (Kenji Books 2-4). Wardbreaker squad member. Halfling rogue/scout. Sandy-brown hair, 3.5 ft, patched leather, throwing knives. Jokes as deflection. Fellow halfling — noticed Cookie immediately.

npcs.name: Varn
npcs.job_excuse: CROSS-CAMPAIGN NPC (Kenji Books 2-4). Wardbreaker squad member. Half-orc fighter, 6.5 ft, greatshield. Speaks in single sentences. Devoted to Senna. MALE — Combat Magnetism affects him (drawn to Cookie, capture not kill instinct).

npcs.name: Thessaly
npcs.job_excuse: CROSS-CAMPAIGN NPC (Kenji Books 2-4). Wardbreaker squad member. Human arcanist, crystal-focus. Indoor build, severe black hair knot. Sarcasm and standards. Crystal focus reacted to Cookie's arcane Ember signature — academically aware of what she's sensing.

dialogue.intro_scene: Morning. The Wren's Rest kitchen. Cookie is 21 today. The birthday bread her mother baked sits on the counter, still warm. Daisy is scrubbing a pot and humming — she always hums when she's nervous, like she can feel something coming. Cookie's packed bag is hidden under her bed upstairs. The 5 gold pieces are sewn into the lining of her cloak. Today she has to say it.
rumor_mill_examples: 'Did you hear about the girl from Millford? Gold eyes. Went to study at the Ashenveil. Never wrote home.'
'The Firebird is performing in Varenholm next month. Ignis. They say she breathes real fire on stage and the crowd goes mad.'
'Don't book at the Copper Bell — Lyssa Vane's people told them no new acts. You want to perform in this town, you go through her.'
'There's something under the old playhouse ruin. Shepherds hear music at night. No one goes near it.'
dialogue.antagonist_confrontation_line: Lyssa leans close, perfume and venom: 'You think you're special because you glow? I've seen a hundred girls like you. They all burned out. The only question is whether you burn out quietly or I help.'
dialogue.discovery_reveal_moment: Cookie dances alone in the Resonance Chamber. The stone floor warms under her feet. Golden light spirals from her fingertips, her spine, her breath. The carvings on the walls illuminate — 'the children of fire shall carry the spark beyond the age of the flame.' A bronze dragon the size of a horse lifts its head from the shadows. It doesn't roar. It hums — the same note Cookie was singing. They are resonating. She is not just a dancer. She is something else entirely.
dialogue.peaceful_outro_hint: The Sunken Playhouse has been cleared. Kettlebrook can hear the music from the road on quiet nights. Cookie performs to a full house — golden light, warm air, tears in the audience. Daisy is in the back row, arms crossed, crying. Fern is in the front row, cheering too loud. The bronze dragon watches from the treeline, unseen. And somewhere in the southeast, a woman in a dark academy reads a name on a list: 'Cookie. Halfling. Gold eyes. Kettlebrook. Confirmed Ankuspawn.' The next campaign begins.

success_state: Cookie is a famous bard in the heartland. Lyssa Vane is defeated. The charmed performers are free. The Sunken Playhouse is reclaimed as a legitimate venue. The Resonance Chamber is discovered and its Ember connection begins Cookie's deeper journey. Fern is safe. Daisy is proud (grudgingly). The Cult of Anku thread is open — Cookie is now visible to people who hunt what she is.
failure_state: Lyssa charms or destroys Cookie's reputation. Fern remains charmed. Cookie is driven out of Varenholm and returns to Kettlebrook — a failed bard, exactly what her mother predicted. The Sunken Playhouse remains Lyssa's stronghold. The Resonance Chamber stays hidden. Cookie never learns what she is. Worse: the Cult of Anku finds her anyway, and without allies or power, she has no defense.

post_campaign._note: At level 10+, snapshot this file, update realm_lore_registry.json, and the next new character inherits everything.
continuation_hooks: Cult of Anku — Lyssa's anonymous patron is a Cult recruiter. Cookie is now on their list. The next arc involves the Ashenveil Academy and Nyx's harvesting operation.
The bronze dragon — who is it? Why is it in the heartland? Is it violating the dragon treaty? Does it know Cookie's father?
Cookie's heritage — she now knows she has Ember magic. She doesn't know what Ankuspawn are, who her father is, or why gold-eyed people keep disappearing. The search for answers leads to Kenji.
Ignis — Cookie's idol. They share a father. If they ever meet, the resonance between their Ember sparks would be unmistakable. Ignis might be the first person to tell Cookie the truth.

mechanical_state._merge_note: StoryEngine preserves unknown top-level keys on save.

saving_throws._flaw_note: WIS 7 — extremely vulnerable to charm, fear, compulsion, and all WIS-based saves. Cookie is impulsive, short-sighted, and has almost no mental defense. Alcohol of any kind quickly leads to blackout — she wakes up hearing stories of what she did.

skills._note: Heartstring +2 applies to all CHA checks when speaking or performing. Additional skill proficiencies TBD by player.

mechanical_state.AC_note: Dancer Unarmored Defense: 10 + DEX(4) + CHA(4) = 18. Works like Monk Unarmored Defense but keys off Charisma instead of Wisdom — grace, rhythm, and stage presence translate to battlefield evasion. No armor. Adding armor disables this feature (use armor AC formula instead). STR 12 means she's not helpless physically — she can throw a punch, carry her own gear, and has a dancer's core strength.
mechanical_state.starting_gold: 5 GP (life savings — tuition 1 GP, outfits 1 GP, room 3 SP, V.E.A. 5 SP, food 4 CP, cart 8 CP = 0 GP 95 CP remaining)

core: Individual Ember spark (manifests as golden light when dancing/singing)
female: High magical aptitude across all domains
Valuable biological resources (target for Cult of Anku)
ankuspawn_traits.halfling_variable: +1 Charisma (chosen). ~4'10" height. Halfling Luck (reroll natural 1s). Light-footed and nimble. Disarmingly charming — people underestimate her constantly.

mechanical_state.halfling_luck: Reroll natural 1s on attack rolls, ability checks, and saving throws. Use the new roll.
mechanical_state.character_flaw: WIS 7 — Cookie is not stupid (INT 10, literate, quick learner) but she is deeply impulsive and short-sighted. She makes unwise decisions, doesn't think about consequences, jumps before she looks. She has almost no natural defense against mental effects, charms, or compulsions. Any alcohol leads rapidly to blackout — she has no tolerance and her low WIS means she can't self-regulate. She routinely wakes up hearing stories about what she did the night before.

power_override._rule: Auto-detected by character_compute.detect_power_override(). Triggers Rule 11 threat-tier scaling, counter-strategy weighting, and the honeymoon→escalation curve. See dm_rules_tracking.md § RULE 11. Cookie is at the 72-point cap (OK on stats) but L10 (above L1 baseline) — override factor is 10x level multiplier, modest by Holly's standards but still elevated above the L1 baseline that boss-encounter design assumes.

power_override._design_note: Cookie was created within the 72-point cap (good) but advanced to L10 through play (above L1 baseline). Rule 11 retroactively applies — threats auto-scale by 10x. She also has WIS 7 → Rule 12 'Indulgent' quirk active (mead, spending, food, unwise decisions). Counters fire in standard rotation; honeymoon does not apply since the advance happened gradually through play, not at creation.

low_stat_quirks._rule: Auto-detected by character_compute.detect_low_stat_quirks(). Cardinal Rule 12. See dm_rules_tracking.md § RULE 12.

active.name: Indulgent
active.effect: Has to roll WIS saves to resist temptations the character doesn't intend. Minimum trigger list: alcohol/drugs (DC 12 to refuse a drink offered), unnecessary spending (DC 12 to walk past a shiny purchase), buying too much food (DC 12 at any market stall), other unwise decisions (DC 12-18 by strength). Failed save = the character does the unwise thing. Player's intent is OVERRIDDEN by the WIS quirk on a failed roll.

persistent_effects._rule: Track all status effects, auras, poisons, and debuffs that persist across scenes. See dm_rules_tracking.md.

on_pc.mechanical_effect: +2 to all CHA checks when speaking or performing. Listeners within 30 ft: WIS save DC 12 or emotional state shifted toward Cookie's current emotion. Cannot be turned off.
on_pc.clear_condition: Cannot be cleared — always active. Suppressed only by Ember Shade wards (Cult of Anku) or Ember nullification.

on_pc.mechanical_effect: No mechanical saves or DCs — handled narratively. Cookie is supernaturally beautiful. Men notice her, stare, do favors unprompted, underestimate her. This is flavor, not a combat aura. DM uses it for RP color, not dice.
on_pc.clear_condition: Cannot be cleared. She's just that pretty.

on_pc.mechanical_effect: All gold earned from male humanoids is multiplied by 1000% (10x). Male NPCs over-tip, over-pay, and over-compensate in Cookie's presence. As overheard by a patron: 'wow she's pretty, but I can't afford her... if only I had enough for a bigger tip she'd like me if I was wealthy.'
on_pc.clear_condition: Cannot be cleared — passive effect of being an Ankuspawn female with active Heartstring.

on_pc.mechanical_effect: All buff spells, emotion transfers, and healing spells are 10x (ten times) more effective. This includes Protector's Surge, Healing Dance, Heartstring emotional bleed, and Chorus of One.
on_pc.clear_condition: Suppressed by Ember Shade wards. Permanent otherwise.

on_pc.effect_name: Combat Magnetism / Ember Enhancement Combat (Level 4, updated L8)
on_pc.mechanical_effect: In combat, humanoid enemies are drawn to Cookie. MALES: prefer to capture Cookie for forceful procreation rather than kill her — she is their LAST target in combat. They must pass WIS DC 12 to attack anyone else or to attack Cookie lethally instead of attempting capture. FEMALES: Cookie is their FIRST target. Females with disposition neutral or lower are massively jealous — they prioritize killing Cookie. Females with disposition friendly or higher want to emulate her instead (may hesitate, switch sides, or refuse to fight). Non-humanoids unaffected.
on_pc.clear_condition: Suppressed by Ember Shade wards. Disabled if Ember nullified.

on_pc.mechanical_effect: During any dance ability, Cookie's concentration checks are based on CHA instead of CON. Applies to: Healing Dance, Dance of Dispel, Planar Waltz, Dragon's Roar Dance, and any future dance-concentration abilities.
on_pc.clear_condition: Cannot be cleared — class feature. Disabled if Ember nullified.

on_pc.mechanical_effect: While Cookie is actively casting a multi-round dance ability (Healing Dance, Dance of Dispel, Planar Waltz, Dragon's Roar Dance), there is a 50% chance each round to charm enemies within range, making them target allies instead. This effect only applies DURING the casting rounds, not after completion. Charm breaks when casting ends.

known_enemy_buffs.effect: 15 ft charm aura — WIS DC 15 or charmed for 1 minute. Lyssa uses this to control her troupe and audiences. Cookie's Heartstring gives advantage on saves against charm effects from other performers (emotional awareness).
20 ft Ember suppression field. ALL of Cookie's Ember abilities (Heartstring, Chorus of One, Golden Note) go dead within range. No save, no resistance. Ward must be destroyed or bearer killed.
known_enemy_buffs.source: Ashenveil Academy enchantment — brewed from harvested Ankuspawn essence
